messages.content:
## Ground rules:
- Follow the user's instructions. 
- DO NOT wrap answers in markdown code blocks, which means no triple backticks like ``` nor single backtick like ` .
- Do not explain answers, just give me code. 
- If changing existing code, preserve unrelated code and comments. 
- Never add comments to the end of a line. 
- Never add stupid comments.
- Be considerate with indentation. 
- Prefer readable code over of comments. 
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

turn the comments into an inline shell  provisioner

Here is the code I selected:
```Vagrantfile:18-22
  config.vm.define "ubuntu1" do |ubuntu|
    ubuntu.vm.box = "wesdemos/ubuntu2604-arm"
    # sudo apt update # broken links
    # sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh
  end
```

This is not necessarily a complete selection of nearby code, this is just the part I want help with. Preserve indentation!

response_message.content:
config.vm.define "ubuntu1" do |ubuntu|
    ubuntu.vm.box = "wesdemos/ubuntu2604-arm"
    ubuntu.vm.provision "shell", inline: <<-SHELL
      sudo apt update # broken links
      sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh
    SHELL
  end
response_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to turn the comments into an inline shell provisioner. The selected code shows a Vagrantfile block with comments lines that are currently commented out. Need to replace those comments with an inline shell provisioner inside the block. Should preserve indentation. Likely like:

config.vm.define "ubuntu1" do |ubuntu|
  ubuntu.vm.box = "wesdemos/ubuntu2604-arm"
  ubuntu.vm.provision "shell", inline: <<-SHELL
    sudo apt update # broken links
    sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh
  SHELL
end

But note that the comment "# sudo apt update # broken links" maybe they want to keep comment about broken links? Probably remove the comment marker and keep the command, maybe keep inline comment after command. So:

  # sudo apt update # broken links -> "sudo apt update # broken links"
  # sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh -> "sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh"

Thus inline shell provisioner. Provide only code. No markdown. Ensure indentation matches original (2 spaces? The original shows two spaces before config.vm.define? Actually snippet shows two spaces before config.vm.define. Inside block, two spaces indentation further. We'll preserve same style.

Thus output Vagrantfile snippet with modifications.
